system.description:
The creature's Fellowship Characteristic is unnatural. Each time this trait is gained, double its Fellowship Bonus. For example, a creature with Fellowship 41 normally has a 4 Fellowship Bonus. With this trait, its Fellowship Bonus increases to 8.

The trait may be gained multiple times. Each time you apply this trait to Fellowship, the Bonus multiplier increases by 1. For example, one selection multiplies the Fellowship Bonus by ×2, two selections by ×3, and three selections ×4. Note this trait does not modify the creature's movement.

During Opposed Characteristic Tests, on a success, the bonus multiplier is added to the degree of success. Any additional multipliers for Unnatural Characteristics simply improve the existing multiplier by 1.

When taking a Skill Test based upon Fellowship with the Unnatural trait, the base Difficulty of the Test is staged downwards one level for degree of multiplier past normal (to a maximum of +30).

During Opposed Characteristic Tests, where success is achieved, the Unnatural multiplier is added to the degrees of success. In the case of a tie, the participant without the Unnatural Characteristic trait loses the Opposed Test.
